title: Power
short: The rate of doing work or delivering energy, P = A / t, measured in watts (W).
full:
Power is how fast energy is transferred or work is done, P = A / t. The same job can be done with little power over a long time or with great power quickly — a strong motor lifts a load faster than a weak one, though both do the same work.

For motion at constant speed power can also be written P = F·v.

The SI unit is the watt (W); 1 W = 1 J/s. The kilowatt-hour used on electricity bills is a unit of energy (power × time), not power.
